description: References 'examples/walkthrough/' directory which does not exist; example files (WalkthroughComposition.tsx, screens.json) are directly in 'examples/' with no walkthrough/ subdirectory
suggested_fix: Change 'examples/walkthrough/' to 'examples/' in the reference text

description: curl downloads from any URL provided by the caller with no scheme or allowlist validation; intended for Stitch GCS URLs but accepts arbitrary input
suggested_fix: Add a URL prefix check (e.g., assert URL starts with https://storage.googleapis.com/) before passing to curl

description: curl downloads from any caller-provided URL; additionally mkdir -p creates directories along the output path without scope validation
suggested_fix: Validate URL scheme and domain; validate OUTPUT_PATH stays within working directory before mkdir/curl

description: OUTPUT_PATH from $2 is passed directly to mkdir -p and curl -o without checking for path traversal sequences; a path like ../../etc/file would write outside the repo
suggested_fix: Resolve OUTPUT_PATH with realpath --relative-to=. and reject paths that escape the working directory

description: @swc/core is pinned to '^1.3.100' (semver range) rather than an exact version; package-lock.json partially mitigates this but fresh installs without the lock file will resolve to the latest compatible release
suggested_fix: Pin to exact version '1.3.100' in package.json to match what the lock file enforces

description: 'Overview' section (lines 17-27) and 'The Goal' section (lines 26-27) contain near-identical sentences describing the DESIGN.md purpose, creating unnecessary duplication
suggested_fix: Merge the two sections or remove the redundant Goal section

description: 'Add appropriate descriptors' in the Visual style row of the assessment table uses the vague quantifier 'appropriate' without specifying what appropriate means
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete instruction, e.g. 'Add mood adjectives (minimal, vibrant, professional) based on page purpose'

description: No formal Output Format section; the artifacts produced (mockData.ts, component files, App.tsx update) are implied by execution steps but never collected into a declared output section
suggested_fix: Add an '## Output' section listing the files the skill produces and their expected structure

description: 'web_fetch' declared in allowed-tools but never referenced in the skill body; all downloads are performed via scripts/fetch-stitch.sh through the Bash tool
'appropriate display duration' in the Adjust timing step uses the vague quantifier 'appropriate' without specifying duration ranges
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete guidance, e.g. 'Ensure each screen displays for 3-5 seconds (see manifest duration field)'

description: 'use appropriate font sizes' in the Best Practices section uses the vague quantifier 'appropriate' without specifying minimum sizes
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete guidance, e.g. 'use font sizes of at least 24px for body text in video output'

description: No formal Output Format section; the skill's output (component .tsx files placed in components/ui/) is implied through CLI workflow descriptions rather than explicitly declared
suggested_fix: Add an '## Output' section stating what files are created and where they are placed

description: 'web_fetch' declared in allowed-tools but not referenced in the skill body; all interaction goes through shadcn MCP tools or Bash CLI commands
Skill acts as a router to workflow files but declares no output format of its own; a reader cannot determine what the skill produces without reading the referenced workflow files
suggested_fix: Add an '## Output' section summarising the three possible outputs: generated screen via Stitch, edited screen, or DESIGN.md file

description: 'Intelligently route user requests' uses the vague qualifier 'intelligently' without explaining the routing decision criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with the explicit routing table already present in the Workflows section, or remove the adverb

description: 'Prefere' is a typo for 'Prefer' in the Best Practices section
'Add the new page to the global navigation if appropriate' uses the vague quantifier 'appropriate' without specifying the decision criteria
suggested_fix: Specify the condition, e.g. 'Add the new page to global navigation if it is a top-level section listed in SITE.md'

description: stitch-design and taste-design declare the Stitch MCP tool as the literal string 'StitchMCP', while stitch-loop, design-md, and react-components use the glob 'stitch*:*'; if the MCP server registers under a prefix other than 'StitchMCP' the two skills will have no Stitch tools available
suggested_fix: Replace 'StitchMCP' with 'stitch*:*' in skills/stitch-design/SKILL.md and skills/taste-design/SKILL.md to match the repo convention

description: Skill tells the agent to review 'examples/walkthrough/' but that subdirectory does not exist; WalkthroughComposition.tsx and screens.json live directly under 'examples/'
suggested_fix: Change reference from 'examples/walkthrough/' to 'examples/' to match the actual directory layout
